Name: curvatureawareprecisionrouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision-Adaptive Routing for Efficient Deep Learning Training
Short Hypothesis: By integrating curvature-aware precision routing with adaptive precision levels, we can significantly reduce training time and memory usage while maintaining or improving model accuracy.
Related Work: Existing literature on precision-adaptive training and curvature-aware optimization (e.g., Tri-Accel, FedQuAD) has shown promise in improving efficiency and accuracy. However, these approaches often treat precision adaptation and curvature analysis as separate processes. Our proposal aims to tightly couple these concepts in a single, unified framework, using deterministic spectral estimators to dynamically adjust precision levels based on the curvature of the loss landscape.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework called Curvature-Aware Precision-Adaptive Routing (CAPAR) for efficient deep learning training. CAPAR integrates curvature analysis with precision adaptation, dynamically adjusting the precision levels of model parameters based on the local curvature of the loss landscape. By using deterministic spectral estimators, CAPAR ensures numerical stability and reduces computational overhead. We validate CAPAR on standard datasets and architectures, demonstrating significant reductions in training time and memory usage while maintaining or improving model accuracy. Our approach is designed to be resource-efficient and suitable for single-GPU deployments, making it highly practical for both research and industrial applications.

Experiments.Description: Evaluate the impact of curvature-aware precision adaptation on training time and memory usage.
Experiments.Method: Train ResNet-18 on CIFAR-10 using CAPAR and compare with baseline methods (e.g., static mixed-precision, full-precision). Measure training time, memory usage, and validation accuracy.
Experiments.Metrics: Training time, memory usage, validation accuracy

Experiments.Description: Analyze the stability and generalization performance of models trained with CAPAR.
Experiments.Method: Perform multi-seed training and evaluate the variance in validation accuracy. Use PAC-Bayes bounds to quantify generalization performance.

Experiments.Description: Assess the impact of curvature-aware precision adaptation on different layers of the network.
Experiments.Method: Monitor the precision levels of individual layers during training and correlate with curvature metrics. Compare with fixed-precision baselines.

Risk Factors and Limitations: The effectiveness of curvature-aware precision adaptation may vary across different architectures and datasets.
Deterministic spectral estimators may introduce additional computational overhead, which needs to be carefully managed.
The approach assumes that curvature information is a reliable indicator for precision adaptation, which may not always hold in practice.
